channel.title: The Brussels Times
channel.description: Latest posts from The Brussels Times.

item.title: Wildfires: France has never faced a situation like this since World War II, says Macron
item.description: Wildfires have consumed over 35,000 ha of forest in France, more than at any time in its history since World War II, says Prime Minister Emmanuel Macron.

item.title: Water shortage in the Netherlands; extra measures needed due to drought
item.description: Government agencies and drinking water companies are now considering new measures to distribute water.

item.title: EU sends Washington a 'long list' of products with a view to reducing tariffs
item.description: The move follows the trade deal agreed by the EU and the US last summer, known as the Turnberry agreement.

item.title: EU agrees to extend temporary protection for Ukrainians – except for 'men of fighting age'
item.description: At Ukraine's request, new applications from Ukrainian men who are eligible to serve in the country's armed forces will be rejected.

item.title: Meta used AI to help it lay off workers with medical conditions, lawsuit claims
item.description: 26 claimants say Meta, the parent company of Facebook and Instagram, used an internal AI system to help decide who would be laid off.

item.title: Trump threatens to strike power plants and bridges if Iran does not negotiate
item.description: The renewed maritime blockade and the bombing campaign are the largest since the April ceasefire.

item.title: DRC Ebola outbreak: Health workers threaten to strike over unpaid salaries
item.description: Frontline health workers battling the Ebola outbreak say they have not been paid since the crisis began and are threatening to strike.

item.title: The jury is still out on age restrictions on social media in Europe
item.description: The European Commission is preparing proposals on minors’ access to social media, but experts it consulted say an outright ban is not the best solution.

item.title: French Customs seize thousands of fireworks illegally imported from Belgium
item.description: French customs on Monday reported seizing more than 25,000 fireworks illegally imported from Belgium at a shop in the Lille area in late June.

item.title: Iran claims to have struck US bases in Jordan, Bahrain, and Kuwait
item.description: The exchange followed a new wave of US bombardments against Iran, in which at least one person was killed.

item.title: At least one Belgian has lost his life in the fires in Andalusia
item.description: In total, 12 people have died while attempting to flee the blaze near Bédar.

item.title: World Cup: England defeats Norway and advances to semifinals
item.description: Their next opponents will be Argentina, who won over Switzerland 3-1.

item.title: Ten dead in Bahamas plane crash operated by local airline
item.description: The crash happened in north Andros, the largest island in the Bahamian archipelago.

item.title: Man nearly sucked out of Ryanair plane after take-off
item.description: "Fortunately, he had not removed his seatbelt."

item.title: Belgians missing after deadly wildfires in southern Spain
item.description: At least 12 people have died, eight are injured, and 23 remain missing.

item.title: Wally Funk, oldest woman to go into space, dies aged 87
item.description: Funk flew into space with Jeff Bezos in 2021, setting a world record at the age of 82 as the oldest woman to have ever been in space.

item.title: Eight men face life in prison over foiled White House birthday attack
item.description: The men have been charged over an alleged plot to attack Donald Trump’s 80th birthday celebration at the White House.

item.title: France beat Morocco 2-0 to reach World Cup semis
item.description: The win sent Les Bleus into the semi-finals, where they will face either Spain or Belgium.

item.title: EU weighs up tougher trade restrictions on illegal Israeli settlements
item.description: The proposals are reportedly contained in an "options paper" circulated to member states.

item.title: 'The EU will always defend its member states,' says Commission after Trump threatens Spain
item.description: Speaking before a meeting of the 32 NATO leaders in Ankara, Trump sharply criticised Spain, calling it a “lost cause”.
